questions.question: In the capstone's research-and-report system, why does paper summarization go through A2A rather than an MCP tool call?
options: A2A is faster than MCP
The writer is an opaque agent — the caller sends a Task and sees only state transitions and outputs, never the writer's internal reasoning
MCP cannot transfer PDFs
A2A is required for any text generation
questions.explanation: Delegation to a specialized agent is agent-to-agent collaboration: the writer keeps its internals private (opacity is verified in the capstone), while tools like arxiv_search stay on MCP. The two protocols are complementary layers of the same system.

questions.question: Why is generate_report task-augmented while arxiv_search is a plain synchronous tool?
options: Reports are larger than search results
Report generation is long-running — the client gets a task id, polls tasks/status, and fetches the result later; search is fast enough to run synchronously
Tasks are required for all tools in production
arxiv_search predates the Tasks spec
questions.explanation: The >30-seconds rule from the Tasks lesson: long work becomes call-now-fetch-later (taskSupport: required) and even returns a ui:// resource on completion, while fast pure tools stay synchronous (taskSupport: forbidden).

questions.question: How does the capstone's security posture express RBAC?
options: All authenticated users get all tools
Per-user policy at the gateway: alice with research:write can call everything; bob with research:read cannot call generate_report
Each tool checks a hard-coded password
RBAC is delegated to the LLM's judgment
questions.explanation: The gateway maps OAuth identities to scopes and gates tools accordingly, alongside pinned description hashes (rug-pull defense) and a Rule of Two audit ensuring no tool combines untrusted input, sensitive data, and consequential action.

questions.question: How can a user click a paper in the rendered report and trigger a new summarization?
options: The report links out to arXiv's website
The ui:// dashboard runs in a sandboxed iframe and posts host.callTool('summarize_paper', {arxiv_id}) back to the host via the postMessage JSON-RPC
The user must copy-paste the id into chat
The report regenerates fully every minute
questions.explanation: MCP Apps closes the loop: the interactive dashboard returned by the completed task is itself a client of the server's tools, calling back through the origin-pinned postMessage channel within its declared permissions.

questions.question: What makes the whole capstone system drivable by other agents?
options: A REST API with Swagger docs
Packaging: AGENTS.md for project conventions plus a run-research SKILL.md wrapping the top-level workflow — any skills-compatible agent can load and operate the stack
A Dockerfile alone
Hard-coded support for one specific client
questions.explanation: The capstone ships the full Phase 13 layer cake: MCP for tools, gateway for security, A2A for delegation, OTel for observability, and the AGENTS.md + SKILL.md bundle so the system itself becomes a portable, agent-operable artifact.
